title: IRS Code 480 Meaning (Offer in Compromise Pending) – For Tax Professionals
description: IRS Code 480 means an Offer in Compromise is pending or under consideration. Learn what 480 signals, what to monitor during review, and how to communicate next steps to clients.
content:
IRS Transcript Codes • Built for 750,000+ U.S. Tax Professionals

IRS Code 480 Meaning

IRS Code 480 flags an Offer in Compromise (OIC) as pending on the module. It means the IRS has acknowledged the offer and is actively reviewing it, but the final outcome has not posted yet.

Try the parser (requires credits)

What is IRS Code 480?

Plain-English definition: Code 480 means the IRS has a pending Offer in Compromise on file for the module and is evaluating whether to accept, reject, or return it.

Common transcript type
Account Transcript
Usually appears near
Codes 520, 971, and other collection/notice activity
Typical context
OIC received, controlled, under review
Client-friendly summary
“Your offer is being reviewed”

When does Code 480 appear?

  • After IRS logging: After the IRS receives and logs a valid OIC for the module.
  • Across covered periods: It may post on each period included in the offer.
  • During consideration: It stays relevant until the IRS posts a decision-related action or the offer is otherwise resolved.

What does Code 480 usually signal?

  • Active review: The offer is in the IRS decision process.
  • Expect notices: You may see notice entries around the same time as information requests or status updates.
  • Outcome pending: The transcript still needs later activity to show acceptance, rejection, or return.

What should a tax professional do next?

  1. Confirm coverage: Verify which periods and liabilities the OIC includes.
  2. Track deadlines: Calendar information requests and response windows tied to notices.
  3. Watch for status changes: Review new transcript entries that indicate acceptance, rejection, return, or additional requirements.

Example: how this looks in a parsed report

Below is a simplified view of how Code 480 can appear in a client-ready transcript report:

Offer in Compromise timeline example

Date: 04/15/2024
Code 480 – Offer in Compromise pending

Code 971 – Notice issued
Date: 07/15/2024

FAQ

What does IRS Code 480 mean on a transcript?

IRS Code 480 means an Offer in Compromise (OIC) has been received and is pending or under consideration for the module. It is not a decision posting.

Does Code 480 stop IRS collection activity?

It often corresponds with reduced collection activity while the offer is considered, but verify the client’s notices, deadlines, and specific account status rather than assuming everything is paused.

How do I turn this into a client deliverable?
